capital.title: An eyebrow-raising set of results for the Gulf sceptics
capital.description: Results from some of the big corporate players in the Gulf have flowed in this week. They were perhaps not what we were expecting. In the UAE, developer Emaar said UAE property sales in the first quarter were up over a fifth year on year – despite a month of conflict in those three. Profit […]

capital.title: Jazeera Airways turns to loss after Kuwait airport closure
capital.description: Kuwait-listed Jazeera Airways swung to a loss after revenue fell in the first quarter due to the closure of Kuwait International Airport. The airport was closed for 57 days, the longest closure of any airport in the region, after an Iranian drone hit the radar system on March 15. The airline reported a net loss […]

capital.title: Drake & Scull revenue more than doubles on project execution
capital.description: Drake & Scull International (DSI) said revenue more than doubled in the first quarter, supported by the accelerated execution of projects across its infrastructure and water treatment portfolios. The top line surged 152 percent to AED77 million ($21 million) in the quarter ended March, compared to AED30 million a year earlier, the Dubai-listed engineering and […]

risk.title: Crisis is a ‘call to double down’, says Adnoc Drilling CFO
risk.description: Adnoc Drilling is accelerating the expansion of its Gulf fleet despite the geopolitical crisis – and calling for global investment in oil and gas capacity, its chief financial officer has told AGBI. The Abu Dhabi-listed company has accelerated rig deliveries in the UAE, Oman and Kuwait since the Emirates left Opec on May 1. The […]

risk.title: Dubai Taxi buys rival for $395m
risk.description: Dubai Taxi Company, a subsidiary of the emirate’s transport regulator, has agreed to buy National Taxi, which operates in Dubai, Abu Dhabi and Al Ain. The deal is valued at AED1.45 billion ($395 million). It will be funded through new bank debt facilities without any new equity issuance, DTC said in a statement to the […]
